TypeError: Got an unexpected keyword argument 'dim'

llm_operation.reason: PyTorch torch.index_select requires 'index' to be a 1-D tensor of valid indices within [0, input.size(dim)), but the original test case does not specify index values — only shape/dtype — causing 'index out of range' when uninitialized. TensorFlow tf.gather does not accept 'dim' as a keyword argument; instead it uses 'axis' (and defaults to 0), and expects 'indices' (not 'index') as parameter name. Both frameworks require concrete index values (not just shape) for execution. Repair: (1) Add valid index values ([0, 1]) within bounds for dim=0 of input shape [3,4]; (2) Rename 'index' → 'indices' in TF case; (3) Replace 'dim' with 'axis' in TF case per doc; (4) Ensure index/indices is int64 (LongTensor equivalent) and values are in [0, 3) for dim=0.
